channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Pair jailed for manslaughter of man shot in head
item.description: Two people have been jailed for their role in the shooting death of 25-year-old Thor Morgan, who was attacked in a suburban Toowoomba street in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Grace Nakamura

media:group.media:description: Thor Morgan died in May, two months after being shot in the head.

item.title: Stephen Colbert wraps up The Late Show with Paul McCartney and a fake pope
item.description: While Colbert's CBS network came in for plenty of jibes over the course of the episode, it was a largely apolitical affair, with attacks on US President Donald Trump ditched in favour of joy, jokes and songs.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Thorpe

media:group.media:description: Stephen Colbert addresses the audience at the start of his final Late Show episode.

item.title: China and Russia block conservation bid for endangered emperor penguin
item.description: The annual meeting of Antarctic Treaty nations has failed to reach consensus to designate the endangered emperor penguin as a "Specially Protected Species".
item.dc:creator: Jano Gibson

media:group.media:description: Conservation group, WWF said the Emperor penguin needed urgent protection

item.title: 'How are you, mate?': Bodycam footage shown of moment police officer was gunned down
item.description: A court hears that some of the last words uttered by a policeman was to ask the man who fatally shot him, "how are you, mate?"
item.dc:creator: Sandy Powell

media:group.media:description: Keith Smith and Leigh Sushames composite image.

item.title: 'Arc de Trump' is one step closer to being built. Here's why it's controversial
item.description: The Arc de Triomphe-inspired arch in Washington DC is meant to commemorate the US's 250th anniversary but its location is causing controversy.
item.dc:creator: Hanan Dervisevic

media:group.media:description: An artist's impression of the proposed "Arc de Trump". Remember the lions have now been removed.

item.title: Man on the run after reportedly raping elderly woman in her own home
item.description: A man who police say raped an elderly woman in her own home on Wednesday night before fleeing the scene remains on the run while investigators appeal for information.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Barrett Peters

media:group.media:description: Police are appealing for information after a man reportedly raped an elderly woman in Palmerston on Wednesday and fled the scene.

item.title: Police probing 70s cold cases to investigate links to knife, shovel and dress at campsite
item.description: Police will review the testimony of a former youth camp manager who found a number of suspicious items in bushland around the time of multiple disappearances near Newcastle in the 1970s.
item.dc:creator: Giselle Wakatama

media:group.media:description: The Crangan Bay holiday campground was popular in the 1960s and 70s.

item.title: From two-way radio to satellite, School of the Air celebrates 75 years
item.description: The world's oldest school of the air is celebrating its 75th anniversary in Alice Springs. It provides a service to families over 1.3 million square kilometres and also is known as the world's largest classroom.
item.dc:creator: Charles Geary

media:group.media:description: The Alice Springs School of the Air delivered its first lessons in 1951.

item.title: Ponga explains why he's in an Origin sweet spot after controversial selection
item.description: Kayln Ponga was a controversial selection for the Maroons, but the Newcastle star says he's backing himself to perform.

media:group.media:description: Kalyn Ponga passes the ball during a Queensland Maroons training session at Sunshine Coast Stadium.

item.title: Media shut out of court during bid to block release of corruption report
item.description: A secret party is trying to block the release of a report from Victoria's corruption watchdog, which examined allegations involving the state government and firefighters.
item.dc:creator: Kristian Silva and Richard Willingham

media:group.media:description: IBAC's probe into corruption allegations began in 2018.

item.title: 36ers eye dream team as former Perth coach reunites with Bryce Cotton
item.description: One of Australian basketball's most successful coaches and one of the national league's greatest players have paired in Adelaide.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Kagi

media:group.media:description: Adelaide 36ers player Bryce Cotton and new coach Trevor Gleeson.

item.title: Diphtheria outbreak could be 'contained within months'
item.description: Rapid vaccination could stem the spread of the potentially deadly diphtheria outbreak, but the government's response has been criticised as being too slow.
item.dc:creator: Olivana Lathouris

media:group.media:description: Rapid vaccination could stem the spread of the potentially deadly diphtheria outbreak.

item.title: Man denied bail after allegedly building explosives in rural home
item.description: A Bathurst man has been charged with explosives and weapons offences as specialist police teams continue to search his home in a quiet regional street.
item.dc:creator: Lani Oataway and Micaela Hambrett

media:group.media:description: Personnel in hazmat suits and masks have been entering and leaving the home throughout the operation.

item.title: US prison officials call off prisoner's execution after 'botched' attempt
item.description: Corrections officials in Tennessee halt an inmate's execution by lethal injection after medical staff repeatedly fail to find a vein.

media:group.media:description: Tony Carruthers has been convicted of murdering three people in 1994.

item.title: Man charged with making threats to kill on Melbourne's Bourke Street
item.description: Police said there was no broader threat to the community on Thursday night, despite the City of Melbourne sending a text message to its staff advising them to avoid the area.

media:group.media:description: A Victoria Police car in Melbourne.

item.title: Premier's fracking warning over possible gas shortfall sparks outrage
item.description: Roger Cook raises the prospect of gas fracking in WA's Kimberley region, warning the state could face future energy shortfalls if Woodside Energy's massive Browse project does not proceed.
item.dc:creator: Michael Tetlow and Keane Bourke

media:group.media:description: Roger Cook warns fracking may be needed in the Kimberley if Woodside's Browse gas project fails to gain approval.

item.title: Why Kotoni Staggs is making his Origin battles personal
item.description: When Kotoni Staggs takes the field he transforms into the angriest man in the NRL and upon his return to the New South Wales side the Brisbane centre is ready to channel his rage into State of Origin.
item.dc:creator: Nick Campton

media:group.media:description: Kotoni Staggs has been recalled to the NSW Blues team for the first time since his debut four years ago.

item.title: Written off as 'parasites', young Indians back a cockroach in politics
item.description: A satirical political party in India amasses the support of millions in just a few days and politicians are taking notice.
item.dc:creator: Claire Campbell

media:group.media:description: An AI-generated image used to promote the Cockroach Janta Party.

item.title: Union executive Michael Crosby takes over as CFMEU administrator
item.description: Mr Crosby replaces outgoing barrister Mark Irving KC, who announced his resignation last month.

media:group.media:description: A new administrator has been appointed to head the Construction, Forestry and Maritime Employees Union.

item.title: 'We adored him': Family's heartfelt tribute after Australian's Inca Trail death
item.description: Australian man Matthew Paton, a Victoria Police sergeant, has died in Peru after falling up to 400 metres down an embankment while walking the Inca Trail to Machu Picchu.
item.dc:creator: Luke Cooper and Andi Yu

media:group.media:description: Matthew Paton has been remembered by his colleagues as a "wonderful, caring person".

item.title: Aerial shooting to resume as brumby numbers spike in national park
item.description: Wild horse numbers in the NSW Snowy Mountains are back up with a reproductive control trial now being considered as aerial shooting has been confirmed to resume.
item.dc:creator: Adriane Reardon and Eddie Williams

media:group.media:description: The 2025 survey estimates between 6,476 and 16,411 remain in Kosciuszko National Park.

item.title: Farm worker found not guilty of circular saw amputation murder
item.description: The jury has returned its verdict in the case of a man who killed pensioner Kalman Tal. The 66-year-old died shortly after paying $5,000 to have his leg amputated in a park.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Testa

media:group.media:description: John Yalu, 41, used a circular saw to amputate Kalman Tal's leg in February 2022.

item.title: Doctors push for e-bike ban in SA for riders aged under 16
item.description: South Australia's peak medical body is calling for reforms to current e-bike laws in the state, including a ban on children aged under 16 from riding them.
item.dc:creator: Josephine Lim

media:group.media:description: Doctors want a ban on e-bikes for children under 16 years old.

item.title: Do robotic lawnmowers actually work and are they worth the investment?
item.description: Experts say robot lawnmowers are becoming more accessible, but there are some key features to look out for.
item.dc:creator: Amy Sheehan

media:group.media:description: Robot lawnmowers can cost several thousand dollars, depending on the type of technology they use.

item.title: Another four platypuses re-homed into Royal National Park
item.description: There are at least 20 platypuses now living in the NSW Royal National Park's waterways, three years after their historic reintroduction.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Chandler

media:group.media:description: Seventeen adult platypuses have been re-homed into the Royal National Park's Hacking River since the project began in 2023.
